title: The dual-write problem
description: Writing to the database and publishing to a broker as two steps cannot be made atomic and will eventually diverge.
body: Use when code commits a DB change and then publishes an event (or vice versa). The two systems have no shared transaction, so a crash between them leaves either a committed change with no event or a published event with no change. Do not try to fix this with try/catch ordering or a distributed transaction across DB and broker. Actions: use the outbox pattern (event row in the same transaction, relayed separately) or change-data-capture so a single commit is the source of truth. Validation signals include a crash-injection test showing no orphaned event or lost event. Risk boundary: a dual write on a critical flow guarantees eventual divergence and is a blocking correctness finding.
